paper_title: Branch, or Layer? Zeroth-Order Optimization for Continual Learning of Vision-Language Models

vision_language_models.zscl: introduces unlabeled reference dataset for knowledge distillation
vision_language_models.mod_x: maintains consistency in multimodal representation spaces
vision_language_models.moe4adapter: uses Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) strategies (peft, peft1, peft2)

optimization_strategies.zeroflow: compares FO optimization strategies and finds ZO advantages in alleviating catastrophic forgetting

investigations.name: Investigation 1: ZO and FO collaboration across branches
investigations.implementation: ZO in either visual or linguistic modality branch while FO is retained in the other
investigations.outcome: significantly outperforms the naive full-ZO approach

investigations.name: Investigation 2: ZO and FO collaboration within branch layers
investigations.implementation: ZO deployed in specific layers (e.g., odd or consecutive layers) within a single branch while FO is kept in remaining layers
investigations.outcome: yields better results, with certain configurations surpassing the full-FO baseline
investigations.mechanism: FO provides stable directivity while ZO's limited volatility helps escape local optima
investigations.observation: visual branch exhibits stronger fluctuations under ZO than language branch
investigations.improvement: stabilization mechanism proposed for the visual branch to constrain ZO's search range

title: Branch, or Layer? Zeroth-Order Optimization for Continual Learning of Vision-Language Models
